compiled_state.title: RED test: Write failing tests for sprint-pause-state.sh
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Testing Mode
RED

## TDD Requirement
Write tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh with 11 failing tests before sprint-pause-state.sh exists. Tests must fail (RED) because the script does not yet exist.

## Test Approach (Given/When/Then)
- **Given**: A temp directory for /tmp state files substituted via env var SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR; epic-id "test-epic-42"
- **When**: sprint-pause-state.sh is called with each subcommand (init, write, read, is-fresh, cleanup, stale-cleanup, resume-context) or sourced for SIGURG handler
- **Then**: State file created at correct path with correct JSON schema, read returns JSON, cleanup removes file, stale-cleanup removes old files, SIGURG handler sets in_progress_marker=false without removing file, resume-context outputs next unanswered story, flag-off noop exits 0 with no file created

## Tests to Write
1. test_spause_script_exists_and_executable — script file exists at plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh and is executable
2. test_spause_init_creates_state_file — init subcommand creates /tmp/sprint-pause-state-<epic-id>.json with fields: epic_id, stories, story_answers, in_progress_marker, created_at
3. test_spause_write_updates_story_answers — write subcommand updates story_answers field in state file
4. test_spause_read_returns_json — read subcommand prints valid JSON to stdout
5. test_spause_is_fresh_absent_returns_nonzero — is-fresh returns exit 1 when file absent
6. test_spause_is_fresh_fresh_returns_zero — is-fresh returns exit 0 when file just created
7. test_spause_cleanup_removes_file — cleanup subcommand removes the state file (exit 0)
8. test_spause_stale_cleanup_removes_old_files — stale-cleanup removes state files older than TTL; fresh files are preserved
9. test_spause_sigurg_handler_sets_recoverable_state — sourcing script and calling _spause_sigurg_handler sets in_progress_marker to false and leaves state file present
10. test_spause_resume_context_outputs_unanswered_story — resume-context subcommand with existing state file outputs the first story whose story_answers entry is absent
11. test_spause_flag_off_noop — when SPRINT_PAUSE_ENABLED=false is exported, init subcommand exits 0 with no file created

## .test-index Update Required
Add RED marker to .test-index:
  plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh: tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh [test_spause_init_creates_state_file]

## Implementation Notes
- Use SPRINT_PAUSE_STATE_DIR env var to override /tmp so tests do not pollute real /tmp
- Each test creates its own TMPDIR subdir for isolation
- Source tests/lib/assert.sh
- Script path: plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh (relative to PLUGIN_ROOT)

## Acceptance Criteria
Universal:
- [ ] bash tests/run-all.sh passes (exit 0) OR targeted test file passes
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED: 0" && echo "pass"
- [ ] ruff check plugins/dso/scripts/*.py tests/**/*.py passes (no .py touched by this task, so skip)
  Verify: echo "N/A - no Python files modified"

Task-specific:
- [ ] tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh exists and is executable
  Verify: test -x $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh
- [ ] Test file contains at least 11 test functions
  Verify: grep -c "^test_spause_" $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh | awk '{exit ($1 < 11)}'
- [ ] All 11 tests FAIL before sprint-pause-state.sh exists (RED phase)
  Verify: bash $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/tests/scripts/test-sprint-pause-state.sh 2>&1 | grep -q "FAILED: [1-9]"
- [ ] .test-index updated with RED marker for plugins/dso/scripts/sprint-pause-state.sh
  Verify: grep '\[test_spause_init_creates_state_file\]' $(git rev-parse --show-toplevel)/.test-index
